stem: A population of bacteria is modeled by the function $P(t) = 800 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^t$, where $t$ is the number of hours elapsed. What is the value of $P(3)$?
explanation:
Substitute $t = 3$ into $P(t) = 800 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^t$:
$$P(3) = 800 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^3 = 800 \cdot \frac{1}{8} = 100.$$

The population after 3 hours is $\boxed{100}$.

distractorRationale.A: Uses $t = 1$ instead of $t = 3$: $P(1) = 800 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 400$. This corresponds to computing only one half-life.
distractorRationale.B: Uses $t = 2$ instead of $t = 3$: $P(2) = 800 \cdot \frac{1}{4} = 200$. This corresponds to applying the decay factor only twice.
distractorRationale.D: Uses $t = 4$ instead of $t = 3$: $P(4) = 800 \cdot \frac{1}{16} = 50$. This corresponds to applying the decay factor four times instead of three.
